notes: GoodRx has a partnership with TrumpRx for Ozempic, but you can get the coupon through GoodRx's site. The website also promotes Ozempic as low as $25 with insurance, but that takes you to Ozempic's website where you have to answer enrollment questions, and then your eligibility is determined. You cannot use the coupon at all if you're enrolled in a government program. To access the coupon you have to confirm that you're not enrolled in a government program and agree to not seek reimbursement from your insurance plan after utilizing the coupon.

The price is the same across all pharmacies because it's a manufacturers coupon that GoodRx is sponsored by. You don't have to register for the coupon but it does clearly state that you cannot be enrolled in a government program, and it makes you confirm that you are not enrolled in any of those programs. Examples of those excluded programs are included and clearly stated, so users don't have to guess.
Some of the offers displayed are the prices available to GoodRx Gold members, but you can still access the free coupons as well.
Promotes free membership where you can get additional savings, but you can get the coupon without registering. Does not disclose that the coupon cannot be using in combination with insurance.
